article_title: AWS Aurora Serverless Gets a 45% Faster Scaling Engine and Up to 30% Higher Throughput in Platform Version 4

content_preview.title: AWS Aurora Serverless Gets a 45% Faster Scaling Engine and Up to 30% Higher Throughput in Platform Version 4
content_preview.summary: Amazon Aurora Serverless platform version 4 scales 45% faster and delivers up to 30% better throughput at no extra cost, targeting agentic AI workloads.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Amazon Web Services on April 20, 2026 shipped platform version 4 of Aurora Serverless, its on-demand, auto-scaling configuration for the Aurora relational database service, delivering up to 30% better performance and a 45% improvement in scaling speed, according to the [AWS Database Blog](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/database/aurora-serverless-faster-performance-enhanced-scaling-and-still-scales-down-to-zero/). The update is available at no additional cost, and all new Aurora cluste...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article covering Aurora Serverless v4 with good technical depth. Includes Overview, What Changed, Benchmark Results, Agentic AI framing, Industry Reaction, Upgrade Path, and What We Don't Know sections. Word count (654) fits News category range.
editor_notes.source_verification: source-0 (aws.amazon.com/whats-new): Official announcement page, confirms the update and April 20 2026 date. source-1 (AWS Database Blog): Primary technical source — confirmed 45% faster scaling (22 min vs 40 min from 0.5 to 256 ACU), HammerDB TPROC-C benchmark (27-34% NOPM improvement), Sysbench read-only (27% faster, 28% lower cost vs v3), Sysbench write-only (32.9% faster, 5.1% lower ACU consumption), authors Jiaming Yan/Ashok Kurakula/Nashad Safa confirmed. source-2 (InfoQ): Confirmed Corey Quinn and Pini Dibask quotes verbatim; Quinn quote in article is first sentence of multi-sentence quote — ends at natural sentence boundary 'tends to land.' Dibask quote is first clause of longer LinkedIn comment, ending with comma indicating truncation, but the key fact (35% discount, highest of any AWS database service) is accurate.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quantitative claims verified against source-1. Benchmark figures (22 min, 40 min, 27 min/109 ACUs, 37 min/151 ACUs, 32.9%, 5.1%, 27-34% NOPM) all match source text precisely. Quote from Pini Dibask ends mid-sentence (after comma), but the factual content within the quoted portion is accurate. Quinn quote ends at a natural sentence boundary.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The AI/agentic framing is accurately attributed to AWS's own positioning. No editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: New topic not covered in recent articles.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, technically accurate article with all claims verified from authoritative sources. Clean APPROVE.
